#!../lua
-- $Id: testes/all.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice at the end of this file


local version = "Lua 5.4"
if _VERSION ~= version then
  io.stderr:write("This test suite is for ", version,
                  ", not for ", _VERSION, "\nExiting tests")
  return
end


_G.ARG = arg   -- save arg for other tests


-- next variables control the execution of some tests
-- true means no test (so an undefined variable does not skip a test)
-- defaults are for Linux; test everything.
-- Make true to avoid long or memory consuming tests
_soft = rawget(_G, "_soft") or false
-- Make true to avoid non-portable tests
_port = rawget(_G, "_port") or false
-- Make true to avoid messages about tests not performed
_nomsg = rawget(_G, "_nomsg") or false


local usertests = rawget(_G, "_U")

if usertests then
  -- tests for sissies ;)  Avoid problems
  _soft = true
  _port = true
  _nomsg = true
end

-- tests should require debug when needed
debug = nil


if usertests then
  T = nil    -- no "internal" tests for user tests
else
  T = rawget(_G, "T")  -- avoid problems with 'strict' module
end


--[=[
  example of a long [comment],
  [[spanning several [lines]]]

]=]

print("\n\tStarting Tests")

do
  -- set random seed
  local random_x, random_y = math.randomseed()
  print(string.format("random seeds: %d, %d", random_x, random_y))
end

print("current path:\n****" .. package.path .. "****\n")


local initclock = os.clock()
local lastclock = initclock
local walltime = os.time()

local collectgarbage = collectgarbage

do   -- (

-- track messages for tests not performed
local msgs = {}
function Message (m)
  if not _nomsg then
    print(m)
    msgs[#msgs+1] = string.sub(m, 3, -3)
  end
end

assert(os.setlocale"C")

local T,print,format,write,assert,type,unpack,floor =
      T,print,string.format,io.write,assert,type,table.unpack,math.floor

-- use K for 1000 and M for 1000000 (not 2^10 -- 2^20)
local function F (m)
  local function round (m)
    m = m + 0.04999

1 #!/usr/bin/env bash
   2 # run_official_test.sh <test_file>
   3 #
   4 # Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
   5 # with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
   6 # launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
   7 # thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
   8 # PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
   9 # context.
  10 #
  11 # Usage:
  12 #   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
  13 
  14 set -uo pipefail
  15 
  16 ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
  17 cd "$ROOT"
  18 
  19 TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
  20 BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
  21 OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
  22 mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
  23 
  24 if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
  25     echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
  26     exit 2
  27 fi
  28 
  29 TEST_FILE="$1"
  30 if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
  31     echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
  32     exit 2
  33 fi
  34 
  35 if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
  36     echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
  37     exit 2
  38 fi
  39 
  40 BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
  41 COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
  42 OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
  43 
  44 PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
  45 _soft = true
  46 _port = true
  47 _nomsg = true
  48 _U = false
  49 arg = arg or {}
  50 _G = _G or _ENV
  51 if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
  52 '
  53 
  54 { printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
  55 
  56 TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
  57 export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
  58 
  59 run_with_timeout() {
  60     local src_file="$1"
  61     local out_file="$2"
  62     local src
  63     src="$(cat "$src_file")"
  64     if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  65         gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
  66     elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  67         timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
  68     else
  69         ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
  70         local pid=$!
  71         ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
  72         local watcher=$!
  73         wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
  74         local rc=$?
  75         kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
  76         wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
  77         return $rc
  78     fi
  79 }
  80 
  81 run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
  82 
  83 reached_exec=0
  84 grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
  85 
  86 if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
  87     && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
  88     printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
  89     exit 0
  90 fi
  91 
  92 if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
  93     cause="stub"
  94 elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
  95     cause="parse-panic"
  96 elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
  97     cause="codegen-panic"
  98 elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
  99     cause="runtime-panic"
 100 elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
 101     if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
 102         cause="runtime-error"
 103     else
 104         cause="parse-or-compile-error"
 105     fi
 106 else
 107     cause="unknown"
 108 fi
 109 
 110 printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
 111 echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
 112 {
 113     grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
 114         || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
 115 } | head -n 5
 116 echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
 117 exit 1

Build is clean (only pre-existing unused-import warnings). The fix is complete.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs` `cg_discharge_vars` for `ExprKind::Indexed` (line 1426) freed the table and key registers in the wrong order. `cg_free_reg_if_temp` (line 1753) only decrements `freereg` when `reg == freereg - 1`; called with the lower register first, it silently no-ops. So after compiling `a[n-3]` where `a` is in R2 and `n-3` in R3 (freereg=R4), only R3 got freed — R2 stayed "live" with the table value, and the subsequent GETTABLE result was patched into a fresh register R3 instead of overwriting R2. R2 then leaked through the next call's argument list, producing the spurious "table" value seen in `__index` results.

**Fix:** In the `ExprKind::Indexed` arm, free the higher register first (matches C-Lua's `freeregs`):
```rust
if idx_reg > t_reg { free(idx_reg); free(t_reg); }
else                { free(t_reg); free(idx_reg); }
```

**New state:** The minimal repro (`a[i*3]` through a 3-deep `__index` chain ending in a function returning `a[n-3]+4`) now passes all 11 iterations and prints "OK". `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the full events.lua test per the instructions — the mega-loop will re-test it.
